step_by_step_thinking:
(Your turn)

I agree with agent2's conclusion that brachycephaly is primarily caused by fusion of the coronal suture. Early fusion of the coronal sutures limits longitudinal growth along the anteroposterior axis, resulting in a short, wide head shape characteristic of brachycephaly—this aligns well with known pathophysiology in conditions such as Apert and Crouzon syndrome.
